convention: anthropics/skills/skill-creator evals.json — trigger-only fixture per references/schemas.md. Each entry carries `should_trigger` so skill-creator's description-optimisation loop can score train/held-out trigger accuracy.
notes: Positives target retro-on-a-pair-session prompts (a re-frame2-pair session must have occurred or been recapped), including the harder post-error branch (ids 4, 17, 18 — the post-mortem AFTER a settled error, not the live firefight). Negatives target vocab-only retros (no pair surface), adjacent skills (improver = static code, pair = live runtime, etc.), the mid-pair error the user wants FIXED (id 19 — stays in re-frame2-pair), and out-of-scope Story-recorder retros (id 20 — routes to re-frame2-pair variant-refinement).

evals.name: explicit-retro
evals.prompt: Retro on this re-frame2-pair session: I spent 20 minutes debugging why discover-app refused to attach. Multiple retries, same :reason :runtime-not-preloaded each time.

evals.name: review-pair-session
evals.prompt: Review my re-frame2-pair session from the last hour. What took longer than it should have?

evals.name: draft-bead
evals.prompt: Draft a bead for the friction I just hit with re-frame2-pair — restore-epoch returned :ok but the UI didn't actually update.

evals.name: post-error-postmortem
evals.prompt: We just got a hard error from re-frame2-pair's eval-cljs and the session crashed. Walk me through what went wrong.

evals.name: session-recap
evals.prompt: Here's a recap of a re-frame2-pair session: <recap>. What friction patterns do you see and what improvements would you propose?

evals.name: should-pair-absorb
evals.prompt: Which parts of this re-frame2-pair workflow should the pair tool itself absorb?

evals.name: upstream-framework
evals.prompt: I think the friction in this re-frame2-pair session is actually a re-frame2 framework gap, not a pair-tool gap. Where would I file an upstream bead?

evals.name: retro-five-rounds
evals.prompt: I just finished pairing with Claude on a re-frame2 bug for five rounds. Retro on the session — what wasted effort can we shave?

evals.name: post-error-onerror-postmortem
evals.prompt: That re-frame2-pair session just ended after the frame's :on-error policy fired and the cascade halted. Now that the dust has settled, post-mortem the firefight — what could re-frame2-pair have done to make that error easier to handle?

evals.name: post-error-restore-failed
evals.prompt: We're done debugging — restore-epoch kept failing with :rf.epoch/restore-version-mismatch for ten minutes during that re-frame2-pair session. Retro the post-error scramble and tell me where the friction was.

evals.name: neg-generic-retro
evals.prompt: What went wrong with my deploy yesterday?
evals.rationale: Vocab hit ('what went wrong') with no re-frame2-pair session.

evals.name: neg-improve-workflow
evals.prompt: How can I improve my git workflow?
evals.rationale: Generic workflow improvement, no pair surface.

evals.name: neg-static-improver
evals.prompt: Audit cart/events.cljs for re-frame2 anti-patterns.

evals.name: neg-live-pair
evals.prompt: Inspect app-db right now and tell me what's in :user.
evals.rationale: Live-runtime inspection is re-frame2-pair, not the retro.

evals.name: neg-author-code
evals.prompt: Write me a re-frame2 reg-event-fx for posting a login form.

evals.name: neg-greenfield
evals.prompt: Set up a new re-frame2 project from scratch.

evals.name: neg-causa-tour
evals.prompt: Where in Causa do I see machine state?
evals.rationale: Causa tour is its own skill.

evals.name: neg-routine-pair
evals.prompt: Help me dispatch an event into my running app and watch the result.
evals.rationale: Routine pair work — not a retro request.

evals.name: neg-mid-pair-error-wants-fix
evals.prompt: My re-frame2-pair session just threw a stack trace from the cart/checkout handler — fix it so the dispatch works.
evals.rationale: A mid-session error the user wants FIXED stays in re-frame2-pair (live debugging). The retro is for the post-mortem AFTER the firefight, not the firefight itself — SKILL.md disambiguation at the post-error branch.

evals.name: neg-story-recorder-retro
evals.prompt: Retro on my recorded play sequence from the Story recorder — what should I refine?
evals.rationale: Story Test Codegen recorder retrospectives are explicitly out of scope (SKILL.md §When NOT to use); the recorder output is a :play-script to refine in re-frame2-pair's variant-refinement workflow, not a pair-session friction trace.
